steps.title: Recognize the exemplification
steps.content: Sentence 1: Talagrand's research shows that random variables cluster around an average. Sentence 2: presents a concrete coin-flip scenario as evidence of that clustering principle.

steps.title: Match the transition to the relationship
steps.content:
The logical relationship is exemplification: sentence 2 is a specific instance of the general principle in sentence 1. The blank needs a transition signaling "here is a concrete example."
  • Choice A — "In addition,": signals a new, separate point being added; sentence 2 is not a separate point but an illustration. ✗
  • Choice B — "However,": signals contrast; sentence 2 does not contradict sentence 1. ✗
  • Choice C — "For instance,": signals a specific illustration of a general claim; sentence 2 is exactly that. ✓
  • Choice D — "In comparison,": signals a comparison between two distinct things; no comparison is being drawn. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
